calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Approving Modification Of Irrevocable Trust
Off Calendar Apr-30-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sterling Ross for Torun Harrah. Brandon Carr (Trustor) also present.

Mr. Sterling stated that this was a petition under 15409 to modify the Trust for changed circumstances which were unanticipated when the Trust was established, that Mr. Carr did not know that Roman (Son) was going to be disabled and that the Trust would disqualify Roman from eligibility for public assistance (Medical). In addition, Mr. Sterling stated that the incompetent provision in the Trust did not apply to Roman and that he did not receive a response or objection from Medical. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Order Approving Modification Of Irrevocable Trust
Off Calendar Apr-12-2010 Continued To Apr-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Modification Of Irrevocable Trust
Off Calendar Mar-29-2010 Continued To Apr-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sterling Ross for Torun Harrah, et al. (Petitioners).

Mr. Ross presented argument to support his position stating that his client didn't anticipate that hsi chlid would be disabled at the time he set this up, that his client wants to have the Trust modified, and that he wants his son to be eligible for public benefits. The Court stated that there doesn't appear to be a change in circumstance. Mr. Ross responded as set forth on the record. The matter was taken under submission.

Mr. Ross requested that the matter be called. At the second call of the matter, Mr. Ross requested that he be allowed to put his statement in writing; he wants to submit a final document. At the request of Mr. Ross the submission was vacated and the matter was continued to April 12, 2010. Mr. Ross' statement is to be submitted by the close of business on April 12, 2010.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Sonia Wong, CSR#11218. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
